sections.paragraph:
Ἀπελλῆς, οῦ, ὁ (OGI 265, 12; 444, 7; IPriene 248; Philo, Leg. ad Gai. 203ff; Jos., Ant. 12, 270) Apelles, greeted in Ro 16:10. The name was common among Jews (cp. credat Judaeus Apella: Hor., Sat. 1, 5, 100). Ac 18:24 and 19:1 v.l. for Ἀπολλῶς; s. B-D-F §29, 4; 125, 1.—M-M.

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

IPriene IPriene = Die Inschriften von Priene—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 101
